calendar.matters:
Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Katzenstein for Rudolph Koppl. Benjamin Kaplan and Douglas MacLellan for (respondent) Monica Zimmerman who is present in court.

Mr. Kaplan reported that on 6/30/15 the respondent filed a request for fees under P.C. 17211, that opposition was due on 8/6/15 but not filed, that supplemental oppositions have still not been filed, that the petitioner's have already been sanctioned for other issues, that 57 days later petitioner's sent a letter explaining why the supplement was not filed, that it placed the blame on the court, that they served a supplement which supplemented nothing and that it did not include a challenge to 17211. Mr. Kaplan further stated that there were unusual circumstances during the argument on 7/30/15, that Mr. Katzenstein raised the possibility that he violated rule 2.30, that the court requested submission of orders without the parties being given the opportunity to view the orders for form or content, that Mr. Katzenstein suggested sanctions in the amount of $2000.00, that the court increased the amount to $4000.00, that they are not contesting what the court or Mr. Katzenstein did, cited codes P.C. 11003, CCP 128.5 as well as various cases and stated that Mr. Katzenstein had no good cause to file his petition. Mr. Katzenstein stated that they tried to file the document, that there was confusion regarding the 8/19/15 hearing, that they tried asking the court but received no response, that they did research on P.C. 17211, cited the Kadisha case, that the court found earlier that they did not act without reasonable cause and that CCP 128.5 determines that they did not act without faith. Mr. Kaplan gave his opinion on the Kadisha case, that he had previously requested $66,362.50 in fees, that there is an additional $2400.00 added for preparation of this hearing, that the current amount requested is $68,762.50 + court fees and that they will apply the $4000.00 already paid. The Court stated that the parties are to exchange respective order by August 28, 2015, submit them to the court by September 2, 2015, that a 1 page objection to the order can also be submitted and that the matter will be under submission as of September 2, 2015.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter: Katy Leonard, CSR #11599, 415-398-1889 (Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Strike Of Respondent Monica K. Zimmerman: Specification Of Matters To Be Stricken From The Verified Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Respondent Monica K. Zimmerman S Demurrer To The Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Kaplan & Douglas MacLellan for (respondent) Monica Zimmerman. Andy Katzenstein for Rudy Koppl.

Mr. Kaplan presented the posture of the case and that they were operating under P.C. 1000 as well as oral argument regarding personal service of documents, the time frame for filing objections, explained why the relief sought was not available to the petitioner citing CCP 128.5 and P.C. 17211(a) and that the petitioner had a right to dismiss the case. The Court stated that it felt it was a mistake to focus on the notice issues, that it is not sure the tentative accurately reflects its current thinking, that the crux of the issue is P.C. 128.5 and that there is some confusion in regards to answering and demurring. Mr. Kaplan stated that there is some confusion as to where the court gets the idea that it can give leave to the petitioner to amend, that when a dismissal is filed the petition is dead, that the petitioner cited an unpublished case, that the declarations by Ms. Thorpe and Mr. Glasser were not challenged, that the case is replete with prevarication. Mr. Katzenstein stated that he thought the courts points on 128.7 were correct, explained why the case was filed, addressed the use of the unpublished case stating that as an estate planning attorney he was not aware of the rule, that he did not do the Lexis search himself and was not aware of the warning. The Court asked about the posture of requesting documents. Mr. Katzenstein stated that he was shocked by the animus of opposing counsel, that he requested information but got no response, that his client and family were denied access to their mother and that they had a good faith reason to be concerned as to what was going on. The court asked about the timing of talking and then filing. Mr. Katzenstein stated that they had telephone conversations but were unable to come to a resolution, that they finally obtained the answers that they needed and dismissed the case and stated that P.C. 128.7 is where the sanctions should come from. Mr. Kaplan stated that P.C. 128.7 has a safe harbor provision, that they have prepared 2 documents that includes a list of documents requested and a summary of fees requested and presented copies to opposing counsel and later to the court. Mr. Katzenstein addressed the document stating that the $56,000.00 in requested sanctions is for something that could have been resolved with a telephone call. The Court stated that P.C. 128.5 focus's on the sole intention to cause delay and asked what is proportionate. Mr. Kaplan stated that it is the direct expenses that the party had to spend to defend the case, that it took opposing counsel 14 days to call after receiving the reply papers and then they filed a request for dismissal. The Court stated its concern of imposing sanctions without some sort of ruling, that the merits have nothing to do with the underlying issue, that the confusion is when he decided it was a dead case, and that P.C. 128.5 is a lot about timing and what is reasonable. The Court requested that the parties submit proposed order by August 4, 2015 and that the Demurrer and the Motion to Strike will be under submission as of that time.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Court Reporter: Dana DiBasilio Tognini, CSR #10118, 415-398-1889 (Reported).

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account To Beneficiaries
